title: eupatorium wallichii var. heterophyllum
intro: Eupatorium wallichii var. heterophyllum, also known as Himalayan feverfew or Himalayan eupatorium, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The herbal preparations of Eupatorium wallichii var. heterophyllum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving headaches, and improving overall well-being.
